# solution.py
"""OfficeTaskScheduler - MARBLE Task #5.

A single-file, standard-library-only, thread-safe office task scheduler.

The module exposes a facade ``OfficeTaskScheduler`` (aliased as
``OfficeTaskSchedulerApp``) that wires four services together over a single
shared ``threading.RLock``:

- ``app.auth``          -> AuthService (registration, login, sessions)
- ``app.tasks``         -> TaskService (task CRUD, comments, dashboard)
- ``app.notifications`` -> NotificationService (fan-out notifications)
- ``app.reports``       -> ReportService (office-wide reporting)

All getters/creators return deep copies so external mutation cannot affect
internal state. Time-dependent methods accept an injectable ``now`` for
deterministic testing.
"""

import copy
import enum
import hashlib
import re
import secrets
import sys
import threading
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Enums
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    PENDING = "pending"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"


class Priority(enum.Enum):
    """Task priority. Int values so reports can sort/compare."""

    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3
    URGENT = 4


class NotificationType(enum.Enum):
    """Kind of notification emitted to a user."""

    TASK_ASSIGNED = "task_assigned"
    DEADLINE_UPCOMING = "deadline_upcoming"
    STATUS_CHANGED = "status_changed"
    COMMENT_ADDED = "comment_added"


class NotificationChannel(enum.Enum):
    """Delivery channel for a notification."""

    IN_APP = "in_app"
    EMAIL = "email"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Exceptions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class OfficeTaskSchedulerError(Exception):
    """Base error for all OfficeTaskScheduler failures."""


class ValidationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when input fails field validation."""


class AuthenticationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when credentials or a session token are invalid."""


class AuthorizationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when a caller lacks access to a resource."""


class NotFoundError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class DuplicateError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when a unique constraint (e.g. email) is violated."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Module-level coercion helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _coerce_status(value: Any) -> TaskStatus:
    """Coerce an enum, its value, or a case-insensitive name to TaskStatus."""
    if isinstance(value, TaskStatus):
        return value
    if isinstance(value, str):
        text = value.strip()
        for member in TaskStatus:
            if text.lower() == member.value.lower() or text.upper() == member.name:
                return member
    raise ValidationError("Invalid task status: " + repr(value))


def _coerce_priority(value: Any) -> Priority:
    """Coerce an enum, its value, or a case-insensitive name to Priority."""
    if isinstance(value, Priority):
        return value
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        raise ValidationError("Invalid priority: " + repr(value))
    if isinstance(value, int):
        for member in Priority:
            if member.value == value:
                return member
    if isinstance(value, str):
        text = value.strip()
        for member in Priority:
            if text.upper() == member.name:
                return member
        if text.isdigit():
            for member in Priority:
                if member.value == int(text):
                    return member
    raise ValidationError("Invalid priority: " + repr(value))


_EMAIL_RE = re.compile(r"^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$")


def _is_valid_email(email: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if email has a non-empty local part, domain, and TLD."""
    if not isinstance(email, str):
        return False
    return bool(_EMAIL_RE.match(email.strip()))


def _new_id() -> str:
    """Return a fresh hex entity id."""
    return uuid.uuid4().hex


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Dataclasses
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    """An office user account."""

    id: str
    email: str
    display_name: str
    password_hash: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work that can be assigned, prioritized, and tracked."""

    id: str
    title: str
    description: str
    creator_id: str
    assignee_id: Optional[str]
    status: TaskStatus
    priority: Priority
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime
    comment_ids: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Comment:
    """A comment attached to a task."""

    id: str
    task_id: str
    author_id: str
    text: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Notification:
    """A notification delivered to a user over a channel."""

    id: str
    user_id: str
    type: NotificationType
    channel: NotificationChannel
    message: str
    related_task_id: Optional[str]
    is_read: bool
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.1 AuthService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class AuthService:
    """Handles registration, credential storage, login, and sessions."""

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        """Create the auth service over the shared lock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._users_by_id: Dict[str, User] = {}
        self._users_by_email: Dict[str, User] = {}
        self._sessions: Dict[str, str] = {}

    def register(
        self,
        email: str,
        password: str,
        display_name: str,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Register a new user and return a deep copy of the created User."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            if not _is_valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("Invalid email format: " + repr(email))
            if not isinstance(password, str) or not password:
                raise ValidationError("Password must be a non-empty string")
            if not isinstance(display_name, str) or not display_name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("display_name must be non-empty")
            key = email.strip().lower()
            if key in self._users_by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("Email already registered: " + email)
            user = User(
                id=_new_id(),
                email=key,
                display_name=display_name.strip(),
                password_hash=self._hash_password(password),
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._users_by_id[user.id] = user
            self._users_by_email[key] = user
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def login(self, email: str, password: str) -> str:
        """Verify credentials and return a fresh session token."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(email, str):
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid credentials")
            key = email.strip().lower()
            user = self._users_by_email.get(key)
            if user is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid credentials")
            if not self._verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid credentials")
            token = secrets.token_hex(32)
            self._sessions[token] = user.id
            return token

    def logout(self, token: str) -> None:
        """Invalidate a session token; unknown tokens are a silent no-op."""
        with self._lock:
            self._sessions.pop(token, None)

    def validate_session(self, token: str) -> User:
        """Resolve a token to a deep copy of its User or raise."""
        with self._lock:
            user_id = self._sessions.get(token)
            if user_id is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid or expired session token")
            user = self._users_by_id.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid or expired session token")
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: str) -> User:
        """Return a deep copy of the user with the given id or raise."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._users_by_id.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise NotFoundError("User not found: " + str(user_id))
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def user_exists(self, user_id: str) -> bool:
        """Return whether a user id exists (no raise)."""
        with self._lock:
            return user_id in self._users_by_id

    def _hash_password(self, password: str) -> str:
        """Return a salted SHA-256 hash as 'salt:hexdigest'."""
        salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
        digest = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
        return "{0}:{1}".format(salt, digest)

    def _verify_password(self, password: str, stored: str) -> bool:
        """Re-hash the password with the stored salt and compare."""
        if not isinstance(stored, str) or ":" not in stored:
            return False
        salt, _, digest = stored.partition(":")
        if not salt or not digest:
            return False
        candidate = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
        return secrets.compare_digest(candidate, digest)

    def _require_user_id(self, token: str) -> str:
        """Validate the session token and return the caller's user id."""
        return self.validate_session(token).id


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.2 TaskService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskService:
    """Manages tasks, comments, dashboards, and access control."""

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock, auth: AuthService) -> None:
        """Create the task service over the shared lock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._auth = auth
        self._notifications = None
        self._tasks_by_id: Dict[str, Task] = {}
        self._comments_by_id: Dict[str, Comment] = {}
        self._tasks_by_assignee: Dict[str, Set[str]] = {}
        self._tasks_by_creator: Dict[str, Set[str]] = {}

    def create_task(
        self,
        token: str,
        title: str,
        description: str = "",
        assignee_id: Optional[str] = None,
        priority: Any = Priority.MEDIUM,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a task, optionally assigning it, and return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Task title must be non-empty")
            prio = _coerce_priority(priority)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < now:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline cannot be in the past")
            if assignee_id is not None and not self._auth.user_exists(assignee_id):
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee not found: " + str(assignee_id))
            desc = description if isinstance(description, str) else ""
            task = Task(
                id=_new_id(),
                title=title.strip(),
                description=desc,
                creator_id=caller_id,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                status=TaskStatus.PENDING,
                priority=prio,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=now,
                updated_at=now,
                comment_ids=[],
            )
            self._tasks_by_id[task.id] = task
            self._index_add(self._tasks_by_creator, caller_id, task.id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._index_add(self._tasks_by_assignee, assignee_id, task.id)
                if assignee_id != caller_id and self._notifications is not None:
                    self._notifications._emit(
                        assignee_id,
                        NotificationType.TASK_ASSIGNED,
                        "You were assigned task: " + task.title,
                        task.id,
                        now,
                    )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def assign_task(
        self,
        token: str,
        task_id: str,
        assignee_id: str,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Assign a task to a user; caller must have access. Return a copy."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            if not self._auth.user_exists(assignee_id):
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee not found: " + str(assignee_id))
            old_assignee = task.assignee_id
            if old_assignee is not None:
                self._index_remove(self._tasks_by_assignee, old_assignee, task.id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            self._index_add(self._tasks_by_assignee, assignee_id, task.id)
            task.updated_at = now
            if assignee_id != caller_id and self._notifications is not None:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    assignee_id,
                    NotificationType.TASK_ASSIGNED,
                    "You were assigned task: " + task.title,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(
        self,
        token: str,
        task_id: str,
        deadline: Optional[datetime],
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Set a task deadline (not in the past); return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < now:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline cannot be in the past")
            task.deadline = deadline
            task.updated_at = now
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_priority(self, token: str, task_id: str, priority: Any) -> Task:
        """Set a task priority; return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            task.priority = _coerce_priority(priority)
            task.updated_at = datetime.now()
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(
        self,
        token: str,
        task_id: str,
        status: Any,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Update task status and notify the other party. Return a copy."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            new_status = _coerce_status(status)
            task.status = new_status
            task.updated_at = now
            other = self._other_party(task, caller_id)
            if other is not None and self._notifications is not None:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    other,
                    NotificationType.STATUS_CHANGED,
                    "Task '" + task.title + "' status -> " + new_status.value,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def add_comment(
        self,
        token: str,
        task_id: str,
        text: str,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> Comment:
        """Add a comment to a task and notify the other party. Return a copy."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            if not isinstance(text, str) or not text.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Comment text must be non-empty")
            comment = Comment(
                id=_new_id(),
                task_id=task.id,
                author_id=caller_id,
                text=text.strip(),
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._comments_by_id[comment.id] = comment
            task.comment_ids.append(comment.id)
            task.updated_at = now
            other = self._other_party(task, caller_id)
            if other is not None and self._notifications is not None:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    other,
                    NotificationType.COMMENT_ADDED,
                    "New comment on task: " + task.title,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(comment)

    def get_comments(self, token: str, task_id: str) -> List[Comment]:
        """Return deep-copied comments for a task in chronological order."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            comments = []
            for cid in task.comment_ids:
                comment = self._comments_by_id.get(cid)
                if comment is not None:
                    comments.append(comment)
            comments.sort(key=lambda c: c.created_at)
            return [copy.deepcopy(c) for c in comments]

    def get_task(self, token: str, task_id: str) -> Task:
        """Return a deep copy of an accessible task or raise."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task = self._get_task_or_raise(task_id)
            self._authorize_access(task, caller_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_dashboard(self, token: str, now: datetime = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Return dashboard rows for tasks assigned to the caller."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            task_ids = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(caller_id, set())
            rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
            for tid in task_ids:
                task = self._tasks_by_id.get(tid)
                if task is None:
                    continue
                is_overdue = (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.deadline < now
                    and task.status
                    not in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED)
                )
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "task_id": task.id,
                        "title": task.title,
                        "status": task.status,
                        "priority": task.priority,
                        "deadline": copy.deepcopy(task.deadline),
                        "is_overdue": is_overdue,
                    }
                )
            rows.sort(key=self._dashboard_sort_key)
            return rows

    def list_assigned_tasks(
        self, token: str, user_id: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> List[Task]:
        """Return tasks assigned to the caller (only the caller's own)."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            target = user_id if user_id is not None else caller_id
            if target != caller_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view another user's assignments")
            return self._collect(self._tasks_by_assignee.get(target, set()))

    def list_created_tasks(
        self, token: str, user_id: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> List[Task]:
        """Return tasks created by the caller (only the caller's own)."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            target = user_id if user_id is not None else caller_id
            if target != caller_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view another user's created tasks")
            return self._collect(self._tasks_by_creator.get(target, set()))

    def get_tasks_for_user(
        self, token: str, user_id: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> List[Task]:
        """Return the union of tasks created by and assigned to the caller."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            target = user_id if user_id is not None else caller_id
            if target != caller_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view another user's tasks")
            ids = set(self._tasks_by_creator.get(target, set()))
            ids |= self._tasks_by_assignee.get(target, set())
            return self._collect(ids)

    # Internal helpers -----------------------------------------------------
    def _collect(self, task_ids: Set[str]) -> List[Task]:
        """Return deep copies of tasks for the given ids, deadline-sorted."""
        tasks = []
        for tid in task_ids:
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(tid)
            if task is not None:
                tasks.append(task)
        tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.created_at)
        return [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]

    def _dashboard_sort_key(self, row: Dict[str, Any]):
        """Sort key: priority desc, then deadline asc (None last)."""
        deadline = row["deadline"]
        has_deadline = 0 if deadline is not None else 1
        deadline_val = deadline if deadline is not None else datetime.max
        return (-row["priority"].value, has_deadline, deadline_val)

    def _other_party(self, task: Task, caller_id: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Return the creator/assignee opposite the caller, or None."""
        if caller_id == task.creator_id:
            other = task.assignee_id
        elif caller_id == task.assignee_id:
            other = task.creator_id
        else:
            other = None
        if other is None or other == caller_id:
            return None
        return other

    def _get_task_or_raise(self, task_id: str) -> Task:
        """Return the internal task object or raise NotFoundError."""
        task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task not found: " + str(task_id))
        return task

    def _authorize_access(self, task: Task, user_id: str) -> None:
        """Raise AuthorizationError unless user is creator or assignee."""
        if user_id != task.creator_id and user_id != task.assignee_id:
            raise AuthorizationError("Access denied to task: " + task.id)

    def _index_add(self, index: Dict[str, Set[str]], key: str, task_id: str) -> None:
        """Add a task id to a user-keyed index set."""
        index.setdefault(key, set()).add(task_id)

    def _index_remove(
        self, index: Dict[str, Set[str]], key: str, task_id: str
    ) -> None:
        """Remove a task id from a user-keyed index set."""
        bucket = index.get(key)
        if bucket is not None:
            bucket.discard(task_id)
            if not bucket:
                index.pop(key, None)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.3 NotificationService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class NotificationService:
    """Creates and serves user notifications with IN_APP/EMAIL fan-out."""

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock, auth: AuthService) -> None:
        """Create the notification service over the shared lock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._auth = auth
        self._tasks = None
        self._notifs_by_id: Dict[str, Notification] = {}
        self._notifs_by_user: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}

    def _emit(
        self,
        user_id: Optional[str],
        ntype: NotificationType,
        message: str,
        related_task_id: Optional[str],
        now: datetime,
    ) -> None:
        """Create IN_APP and EMAIL notifications for a user (no-op if None)."""
        if user_id is None:
            return
        for channel in (NotificationChannel.IN_APP, NotificationChannel.EMAIL):
            notif = Notification(
                id=_new_id(),
                user_id=user_id,
                type=ntype,
                channel=channel,
                message=message,
                related_task_id=related_task_id,
                is_read=False,
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._notifs_by_id[notif.id] = notif
            self._notifs_by_user.setdefault(user_id, []).append(notif.id)

    def check_upcoming_deadlines(
        self,
        token: str,
        within_hours: float = 24,
        now: datetime = None,
        within: float = None,
    ) -> List[Notification]:
        """Emit DEADLINE_UPCOMING notifications for the caller's due tasks."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            if within is not None:
                within_hours = within
            horizon = now + timedelta(hours=within_hours)
            created: List[Notification] = []
            if self._tasks is None:
                return created
            task_ids = sorted(self._tasks._tasks_by_assignee.get(caller_id, set()))
            for tid in task_ids:
                task = self._tasks._tasks_by_id.get(tid)
                if task is None or task.deadline is None:
                    continue
                if task.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED):
                    continue
                if now <= task.deadline <= horizon:
                    before_len = len(self._notifs_by_user.get(caller_id, []))
                    self._emit(
                        caller_id,
                        NotificationType.DEADLINE_UPCOMING,
                        "Task '" + task.title + "' deadline is approaching",
                        task.id,
                        now,
                    )
                    for nid in self._notifs_by_user.get(caller_id, [])[before_len:]:
                        created.append(copy.deepcopy(self._notifs_by_id[nid]))
            return created

    def get_notifications(
        self, token: str, unread_only: bool = False
    ) -> List[Notification]:
        """Return the caller's notifications in chronological order."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            result: List[Notification] = []
            for nid in self._notifs_by_user.get(caller_id, []):
                notif = self._notifs_by_id.get(nid)
                if notif is None:
                    continue
                if unread_only and notif.is_read:
                    continue
                result.append(copy.deepcopy(notif))
            return result

    def mark_read(self, token: str, notification_id: str) -> Notification:
        """Mark a notification read; return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            notif = self._notifs_by_id.get(notification_id)
            if notif is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Notification not found: " + str(notification_id))
            if notif.user_id != caller_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot modify another user's notification")
            notif.is_read = True
            return copy.deepcopy(notif)

    def mark_all_read(self, token: str) -> int:
        """Mark all the caller's notifications read; return count changed."""
        with self._lock:
            caller_id = self._auth.validate_session(token).id
            changed = 0
            for nid in self._notifs_by_user.get(caller_id, []):
                notif = self._notifs_by_id.get(nid)
                if notif is not None and not notif.is_read:
                    notif.is_read = True
                    changed += 1
            return changed


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.4 ReportService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ReportService:
    """Computes office-wide task reports for any authenticated user."""

    def __init__(
        self, lock: threading.RLock, auth: AuthService, tasks: TaskService
    ) -> None:
        """Create the report service over the shared lock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._auth = auth
        self._tasks = tasks

    def completion_rate(self, token: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Return total/completed counts and the completion rate."""
        with self._lock:
            self._auth.validate_session(token)
            tasks = self._tasks._tasks_by_id
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks.values() if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            rate = round(completed / total, 4) if total else 0.0
            return {"total": total, "completed": completed, "rate": rate}

    def overdue_tasks(
        self, token: str, now: datetime = None
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Return rows for all overdue, non-final tasks, deadline-sorted."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            self._auth.validate_session(token)
            rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
            for task in self._tasks._tasks_by_id.values():
                if task.deadline is None:
                    continue
                if task.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED):
                    continue
                if task.deadline < now:
                    rows.append(
                        {
                            "task_id": task.id,
                            "title": task.title,
                            "assignee_id": task.assignee_id,
                            "deadline": copy.deepcopy(task.deadline),
                            "priority": task.priority,
                            "status": task.status,
                        }
                    )
            rows.sort(key=lambda r: r["deadline"])
            return rows

    def task_distribution(self, token: str) -> Dict[str, int]:
        """Return a map of assignee id -> task count ('unassigned' bucket)."""
        with self._lock:
            self._auth.validate_session(token)
            dist: Dict[str, int] = {}
            for task in self._tasks._tasks_by_id.values():
                key = task.assignee_id if task.assignee_id is not None else "unassigned"
                dist[key] = dist.get(key, 0) + 1
            return dist

    def summary(self, token: str, now: datetime = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a combined report bundle for the caller."""
        with self._lock:
            now = now or datetime.now()
            return {
                "completion": self.completion_rate(token),
                "overdue": self.overdue_tasks(token, now=now),
                "distribution": self.task_distribution(token),
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Facade wiring
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class OfficeTaskScheduler:
    """Facade wiring auth, tasks, notifications, and reports over one lock."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        """Construct services sharing a single RLock and cross-wire them."""
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self.auth = AuthService(self._lock)
        self.tasks = TaskService(self._lock, self.auth)
        self.notifications = NotificationService(self._lock, self.auth)
        self.reports = ReportService(self._lock, self.auth, self.tasks)
        self.tasks._notifications = self.notifications
        self.notifications._tasks = self.tasks

    def register(
        self,
        email: str,
        password: str,
        display_name: str,
        now: datetime = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Facade pass-through to ``auth.register``."""
        return self.auth.register(email, password, display_name, now=now)

    def login(self, email: str, password: str) -> str:
        """Facade pass-through to ``auth.login``."""
        return self.auth.login(email, password)

    def logout(self, token: str) -> None:
        """Facade pass-through to ``auth.logout``."""
        return self.auth.logout(token)


OfficeTaskSchedulerApp = OfficeTaskScheduler


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Demo
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def main() -> None:
    """Exercise all requirement groups end-to-end and print results."""
    try:
        sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")
    except Exception:
        pass

    base = datetime(2026, 6, 24, 9, 0, 0)
    app = OfficeTaskScheduler()

    # Group 1: register multiple users -----------------------------------
    alice = app.auth.register("alice@example.com", "pw-alice-1", "Alice", now=base)
    bob = app.auth.register("bob@example.com", "pw-bob-1", "Bob", now=base)
    carol = app.auth.register("carol@example.com", "pw-carol-1", "Carol", now=base)
    print("Registered users -> Alice, Bob, Carol")

    # Group 2: login multiple users (distinct tokens) ---------------------
    alice_t = app.auth.login("alice@example.com", "pw-alice-1")
    alice_t2 = app.auth.login("alice@example.com", "pw-alice-1")
    bob_t = app.auth.login("bob@example.com", "pw-bob-1")
    print("Two Alice tokens distinct -> " + str(alice_t != alice_t2))

    # Group 3: create + assign tasks with deadlines + priorities ----------
    t1 = app.tasks.create_task(
        alice_t,
        "Ship release",
        description="Cut the 1.0 release",
        assignee_id=bob.id,
        priority="HIGH",
        deadline=base + timedelta(hours=12),
        now=base,
    )
    t2 = app.tasks.create_task(
        alice_t,
        "Write docs",
        assignee_id=alice.id,
        priority=Priority.MEDIUM,
        deadline=base + timedelta(days=3),
        now=base,
    )
    t3 = app.tasks.create_task(
        bob_t,
        "Fix overdue bug",
        assignee_id=bob.id,
        priority="URGENT",
        deadline=base - timedelta(hours=1),
        now=base - timedelta(hours=2),
    )
    print("Created tasks -> " + ", ".join([t1.title, t2.title, t3.title]))

    # Group 4: dashboard --------------------------------------------------
    dash = app.tasks.get_dashboard(bob_t, now=base)
    print("Bob dashboard rows -> " + str(len(dash)))
    for row in dash:
        print(
            "  - "
            + row["title"]
            + " | "
            + row["priority"].name
            + " | overdue="
            + str(row["is_overdue"])
        )

    # Group 5: status updates + comments ----------------------------------
    app.tasks.update_status(bob_t, t1.id, "in_progress", now=base + timedelta(hours=1))
    app.tasks.add_comment(
        bob_t, t1.id, "Started working on this.", now=base + timedelta(hours=1)
    )
    app.tasks.add_comment(
        alice_t, t1.id, "Thanks, ping me if blocked.", now=base + timedelta(hours=2)
    )
    comments = app.tasks.get_comments(alice_t, t1.id)
    print("Comments on '" + t1.title + "' -> " + str(len(comments)))

    # Group 6: notifications incl. upcoming deadline ----------------------
    bob_notifs = app.notifications.get_notifications(bob_t)
    assigned = [n for n in bob_notifs if n.type == NotificationType.TASK_ASSIGNED]
    print("Bob TASK_ASSIGNED notifications -> " + str(len(assigned)))
    upcoming = app.notifications.check_upcoming_deadlines(
        bob_t, within_hours=24, now=base
    )
    print("Bob upcoming-deadline notifications -> " + str(len(upcoming)))

    # Group 7: reports ----------------------------------------------------
    app.tasks.update_status(
        alice_t, t2.id, "completed", now=base + timedelta(hours=3)
    )
    cr = app.reports.completion_rate(alice_t)
    print(
        "Completion -> total="
        + str(cr["total"])
        + " completed="
        + str(cr["completed"])
        + " rate="
        + str(cr["rate"])
    )
    overdue = app.reports.overdue_tasks(alice_t, now=base)
    print("Overdue tasks -> " + str(len(overdue)))
    dist = app.reports.task_distribution(alice_t)
    print("Task distribution -> " + str({k: v for k, v in sorted(dist.items())}))

    # Group 8: edge-case sanity -------------------------------------------
    checks = []
    try:
        app.tasks.assign_task(alice_t, t1.id, "no-such-user", now=base)
    except NotFoundError:
        checks.append("assign->NotFoundError")
    try:
        app.tasks.set_deadline(alice_t, t1.id, base - timedelta(days=1), now=base)
    except ValidationError:
        checks.append("past-deadline->ValidationError")
    carol_t = app.auth.login("carol@example.com", "pw-carol-1")
    try:
        app.tasks.get_task(carol_t, t1.id)
    except AuthorizationError:
        checks.append("foreign-access->AuthorizationError")
    try:
        app.tasks.get_task("bad-token", t1.id)
    except AuthenticationError:
        checks.append("bad-token->AuthenticationError")
    try:
        app.auth.register("alice@example.com", "x", "Alice2", now=base)
    except DuplicateError:
        checks.append("dup-email->DuplicateError")
    print("Edge cases -> " + ", ".join(checks))
    print("Demo complete.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
